title: Declaring Structs
description: Create and initialize standard C++ structs.

slides.title: Building a Student 👦
slides.content: Let's build a struct representing a 'Student'. They have an ID number, an Age, and a Name.

slides.title: Example Syntax
slides.content: Notice how we define `myStudent1` at the very end of the struct block, before the semicolon.

slides.title: Dot Syntax 🎯
slides.content: To access the members of a struct variable, you use the dot syntax `.`.

slides.title: Assigning Values
slides.content: Let's give our structure some data.

quizData.question: Which operator is used to access the internal 'member variables' of a C++ structure?
quizData.explanation: You "dot" into the structure using `.` to reach its member properties.
